concepts.name: Limit
concepts.short_definition: The value a function approaches as its input approaches a certain value.

concepts.name: Function
concepts.short_definition: A relation between a set of inputs and a set of permissible outputs.

concepts.name: Continuity
concepts.short_definition: A function is continuous if its graph can be drawn without lifting the pen.

concepts.name: Point Discontinuity
concepts.short_definition: A single point where a function is not defined or has a different value.

concepts.name: Jump Discontinuity
concepts.short_definition: A discontinuity where the function 'jumps' from one value to another.

concepts.name: Infinite Discontinuity
concepts.short_definition: A discontinuity where the function approaches infinity or negative infinity.

concepts.name: Intermediate Value Theorem
concepts.short_definition: States that for a continuous function, all values between the function's minimum and maximum are also attained.

concepts.name: Domain
concepts.short_definition: The set of all possible input values for a function.

concepts.name: Range
concepts.short_definition: The set of all possible output values for a function.

concepts.name: Continuity on an Interval
concepts.short_definition: A function is continuous on an interval if it is continuous at every point within that interval.
